originalBehavior: The page interleaves long-form explanatory text with intro and outro vignettes, cartoon cues, and embedded playable sections in a fixed narrative order.
localStatus: Top-level page structure is vendored from upstream; the only top-level additions are metadata updates and the shared reference footer, while the intro translation strip was removed from the public iframe flow and the translator helper was relocated into docs.
notes: The local page keeps the original iframe layout rather than collapsing the content into separate modules.
Narrative rhythm remains owned by the original HTML document.
The intro iframe no longer advertises upstream translation pages, but the playable sequence stays in the same order.
Technical translation tooling now lives under docs instead of the published polygons route.
evidence: Local audit notes emphasize page-level parity rather than module-only replication.
Live page walkthrough cross-checked at https://ncase.me/polygons/ on 2026-03-10.

originalBehavior: Users drag unhappy triangles and squares to random empty spots until no one wants to move, with immediate happiness recalculation after each drop.
localStatus: Manual board remains in the upstream iframe and supporting scripts, loaded under the local subpath without a rewritten host wrapper.
notes: Drop handling and local mood updates still happen inside the original manual module.
The outer replica page does not intercept drag interactions.
evidence: Local audit inventory calls out drag-and-drop neighborhood behavior and immediate happy/unhappy updates.
Upstream manual board lives in its own iframe-backed module.

originalBehavior: Unhappy shapes automatically move to random empty spots over time so users can observe segregation emerge across repeated runs.
localStatus: Autoplay behavior is preserved through the vendored automatic simulation module.
notes: The local copy keeps the original iframe dimensions and runtime structure.
No framework wrapper was added around the autoplay state machine.
evidence: Local audit notes describe repeated autoplay runs and macro-pattern emergence.
Upstream source isolates autoplay into the automatic module.

originalBehavior: Automatic simulations plot a segregation metric over time alongside the board so local moves can be compared with the macro trend.
localStatus: Graph updates remain bundled with the original automatic simulation logic.
notes: Graph rendering and board updates still share the same upstream runtime.
The docs contract tracks the graph separately because it is a parity-critical output.
evidence: Local audit notes explicitly call out the segregation graph module.
Live page review confirmed the graph still appears in the same chapter position.

originalBehavior: Slider-driven sections change the individual bias threshold and visibly shift segregation outcomes once the preference crosses key tipping points.
localStatus: Bias slider sections remain in the upstream automatic variants and shared double-slider utilities.
notes: Separate upstream HTML files preserve the staged narrative about 33 percent and 50 percent thresholds.
Pathing changes were limited to hosting compatibility.
evidence: Local audit notes mention slider threshold changes and tipping-point behavior.
Source tree includes multiple automatic variants dedicated to threshold scenarios.

originalBehavior: Later sections introduce anti-bias or diversity-demand controls that show how even slight active preference for diversity can de-segregate the world.
localStatus: Anti-bias scenarios continue to use the original automatic module variants and slider utilities.
notes: The local replica keeps the original pairing of mini-scene explanation plus full-size simulation.
No separate state bridge was introduced between the mini and automatic sections.
evidence: Local audit notes identify anti-bias controls and de-segregation behavior as a distinct module.
Live observation on 2026-03-10 confirmed the same sequence of mini and full-scale sections.

originalBehavior: A large sandbox at the end lets users freely vary parameters and explore the segregation model after the guided story concludes.
localStatus: Sandbox behavior remains in the vendored upstream iframe, with only the host page footer added externally.
notes: The sandbox stays in its original chapter position after the cooperation section.
Reset and playback behavior remain owned by the upstream simulation script.
evidence: Local audit notes explicitly call out the large sandbox as a final module.
Smoke testing targeted the sandbox route because it stresses the nested iframe pathing.
